headline: Monday, Day 184 — Frankie's PT morning, and this is the one the last three weeks have pointed at. At 8:00 AM PT two doors open at the same instant: the IRS phone line (the RedFax reply window from the Jun 27-28 SS-4 closed Friday with nothing received — so today the EIN gets called for, not waited for) and the HR inboxes that make the zero-cost, zero-gate B2B email finally sendable. Both taps are Frankie's, both are free, both take minutes, and everything else on the board queues behind them. Meanwhile the autoloop re-failed its 09:00 retry again this morning — day 15 frozen at cycle 10, on a 5-minute macOS toggle.

spotlight.title: 8:00 AM PT sharp — call the IRS. The waiting phase is over.
spotlight.detail: Day 184 of the EIN. The ~8-business-day reply window from the Jun 27-28 SS-4 re-fax closed Friday Jul 10 with nothing arriving on the RedFax line. Sequence this morning: (1) 60-second check of RedFax 510-497-1224 — if the IRS faxed back over the weekend you are done, and A2P submits today. (2) If it is empty, call the IRS Business & Specialty line 800-829-4933 the minute it opens at 8:00 AM PT. Expect hold; that is the price. The EIN is the one document A2P → FBN → registered agent → SMS all queue behind — this call is worth more than any other hour on today's board.

spotlight.title: The $0 HR email has an open inbox again — send it before the day fills up.
spotlight.detail: The cheapest path to a first conversation in the whole company: no EIN, no A2P, no TCPA, no Lob funds, no data-broker registration — B2B CAN-SPAM only. It has been one tap away since Jul 6 and every open-inbox morning since has passed unsent. The 6 HR leaders are already researched, cited and pre-filled: Renato Razon (RNDC), Jenni Myles (KBR), Cornelius Boone (eBay), Kelly Russell (Cloudflare), Avani Prabhakar (Atlassian), Scott Withycombe (Snap). Confirm each is still in role, reveal work emails (≤6 Apollo credits), load the 3-touch sequence from 09_b2b_hr_lane/ and send — B2B_GO_LIVE_RUNBOOK.md has the order. If the card is out for Apollo anyway: fund Lob the same minute (~$50-70) and approve the postcard — lob_merge_TIER1.csv is sitting at 77/77 mailable with per-lead QR attribution, the fastest path to a first booked appointment.

spotlight.title: The autoloop failed again at 09:00 this morning. Day 15. It is a 5-minute toggle.
spotlight.detail: launchd.err.log logged another 'Operation not permitted' on oraya-loop.sh at 09:00 IST today — the same macOS Full Disk Access denial on every scheduled run since Jun 28. Fifteen days × 2 cycles/day ≈ 30 autonomous build cycles the company has not had. Fix: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Full Disk Access → add /bin/bash → re-run install-scheduler.sh. While you are in there, the two other no-business-hours unblocks: the 1-min ATTOM key refresh (trial key 401s — new key at api.developer.attomdata.com, overwrite .oraya-credentials/attom_api_key) and the ~$25 BatchData fund that switches on the cold-email lane for the 77 HOF leads.

blockers.label: EIN
blockers.detail: Day 184 — and today it stops being a waiting game. The ~8-business-day RedFax window from the Jun 27-28 SS-4 re-fax closed Friday with no reply on the line, and the connected inbox is live-verified empty (0 threads in 72h — though the EIN answers by fax, not email). Unblock path: Frankie checks RedFax 510-497-1224, then calls the IRS at 800-829-4933 at 8:00 AM PT today; Chitraksh covers the 8–11 AM PT (8:30–11:30 PM IST) rotation if the hold runs long. A2P → FBN → registered agent → SMS all queue behind this one document.

blockers.label: Launch gates
blockers.detail: 5 founder taps still stand between a finished build and first revenue — and today is the first day this week that every one of them is executable. Frankie: send the $0 HR email (inboxes now open) + fund Lob and approve the postcard (~$50-70). Chitraksh: the Full-Disk-Access toggle, the 1-min ATTOM key refresh, the ~$25 BatchData fund. Optional multiplier: fill launch_config.template.env once (20 blanks, ~15 min) — it stamps all three lanes at once.

blockers.label: Autoloop scheduler
blockers.detail: Day 15 frozen at cycle 10. The 09:00 IST retry failed again this morning — 'Operation not permitted' on oraya-loop.sh, the same Full Disk Access denial on every run since Jun 28. Highest-leverage 5 minutes on the board: until it clears, the company gets zero autonomous build cycles per day.
